description: Interactive data visualization website for US traffic accidents (2016-2023) using React + Vite, D3.js, Leaflet.js, and Tailwind CSS. Pre-processes ~1.76M CSV records into static JSON for a two-page app: Explorer (interactive map) and Analytics (dashboard).

userStories.title: Data preprocessing script — summary, yearly, severity, and monthly JSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Python script that reads all 4 CSV files and generates aggregated JSON files for the Analytics dashboard KPIs and basic charts.
acceptanceCriteria: Python script `preprocess.py` reads all 4 CSVs from `US_Accidents_Full2022_parts/`
Outputs `public/data/summary.json` with: totalAccidents, avgDangerScore, mostDangerousCounty (name + score), peakHour
Outputs `public/data/by_year.json` with accident counts grouped by year (2016-2023)
Outputs `public/data/by_severity.json` with accident counts grouped by severity level (1-4)
Outputs `public/data/by_month.json` with accident counts aggregated by month (Jan-Dec) across all years
Danger score formula: (S1*N1 + S2*N2 + S3*N3 + S4*N4) / T
Script runs successfully: `python preprocess.py`
All output JSON files are under 5MB each

userStories.title: Data preprocessing script — county danger, weather, day-hour, and state JSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need additional aggregated JSON files for the remaining Analytics charts and Explorer map data.
acceptanceCriteria: Outputs `public/data/by_county_danger.json` with top 50 counties ranked by danger score, each with county name, state, score, and accident count
Outputs `public/data/by_weather.json` with weather condition distribution: visibility buckets, wind speed buckets, precipitation buckets with counts and percentages
Outputs `public/data/by_day_hour.json` with 7x24 matrix (day-of-week x hour-of-day) of accident counts
Outputs `public/data/by_state.json` with per-state accident counts and danger scores
Script runs successfully: `python preprocess.py`
All output JSON files are under 5MB each

userStories.title: Data preprocessing — chunked map sample data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sampled accident data split into chunks for the Explorer map so data loads progressively with a loading indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Outputs multiple chunk files in `public/data/map/` (e.g., chunk_0.json, chunk_1.json, ...) each ~1-2MB
Each record includes: id, lat, lng, severity, startTime, weatherCondition, temperature, humidity, visibility, windSpeed, description, street, city, state, distance
Outputs `public/data/map/manifest.json` listing all chunk filenames and total record count
Total sampled records ~50K, evenly distributed across severity levels and states
Script runs successfully: `python preprocess.py`
userStories.notes: Chunks enable progressive loading on the Explorer map

userStories.title: React + Vite project scaffolding with Tailwind CSS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project initialized with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and all dependencies installed.
acceptanceCriteria: React + Vite project created in project root with JSX support
Tailwind CSS v3+ configured and working
React Router v6 installed and configured with routes: / (redirects to /explorer), /explorer, /analytics, /about
D3.js, Leaflet, leaflet.markercluster dependencies installed
App runs with `npm run dev` and shows a placeholder page
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Navigation bar and page layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a top navigation bar to switch between Explorer, Analytics, and About pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Top nav bar with site title 'US Accidents Visualization' and subtitle 'CSC 805 Data Visualization Project' on the left
Navigation links on the right: Explorer, Analytics, About
Active page button is filled dark (e.g., Analytics has dark background with white text), inactive buttons are outline/text style
Clicking nav links switches pages without full reload via React Router
Layout component wraps all pages with the navbar
Matches Figma header design
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — KPI summary cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see 4 key statistics cards at the top of the Analytics dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics page renders at /analytics route
4 cards in a horizontal row: Total Accidents, Avg Danger Score, Most Dangerous County, Peak Hour
Each card shows: icon, label, large bold value, and gray subtitle text
Data fetched from `public/data/summary.json` on mount
Loading state shown while data is being fetched
Cards match Figma design (white background, subtle border/shadow, rounded corners)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — Accidents Over Time bar chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want a D3.js bar chart showing yearly accident counts to understand trends.
acceptanceCriteria: D3.js bar chart in a white card on the Analytics page
X-axis: years (2016-2023), Y-axis: accident count (formatted as K/M)
Bars are blue/steel color
Growth badge in top-right corner showing percentage change from first to last year
Chart title: 'Accidents Over Time', subtitle: 'Yearly trend 2016-2023'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_year.json`
Chart resizes properly with container using useRef + useEffect pattern
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — Severity Distribution donut chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want a donut chart showing the distribution of accident severity levels.
acceptanceCriteria: D3.js donut chart in a white card on the Analytics page
4 segments with colors: S1 Minor (green), S2 Moderate (yellow/orange), S3 Serious (orange/red), S4 Fatal (dark red)
Legend below chart showing severity label, color swatch, and percentage for each level
Chart title: 'Severity Distribution', subtitle: 'Across all recorded accidents'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_severity.json`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — Top 10 Counties by Danger Score
userStories.description: As a user, I want a ranked list of the most dangerous counties with horizontal bar indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: Ranked list (1-10) in a white card showing county name and state abbreviation
Each row has a horizontal red bar proportional to the danger score (max bar = highest score)
Danger score value displayed to the right of the bar
External link icon next to each score (links to Explorer page filtered to that county)
Chart title: 'Top 10 Counties by Danger Score', subtitle: 'Click to view on map'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_county_danger.json`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Clicking a county navigates to Explorer filtered to that county

userStories.title: Analytics page — Weather Conditions horizontal bars
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see weather condition distribution as horizontal bars.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontal bar chart in a white card with 3 categories: Visibility, Wind Speed, Precipitation
Each bar shows absolute count and percentage
Color-coded: teal for visibility, blue for wind speed, dark blue for precipitation
Chart title: 'Weather Conditions', subtitle: 'Accident distribution by weather at time of incident'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_weather.json`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — Accident Heatmap by Day & Hour
userStories.description: As a user, I want a heatmap showing accident density by day of week and hour of day.
acceptanceCriteria: D3.js heatmap grid in a white card: 7 rows (Mon-Sun) x 24 columns (12a-11p)
Color scale from light yellow (fewer accidents) to dark red/orange (more accidents)
Color legend at bottom showing gradient from 'Less' to 'More'
Chart title: 'Accident Heatmap by Day & Hour', subtitle: 'Intensity shows accident density — darker = more accidents'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_day_hour.json`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — Monthly Accident Trend line/area chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want a line chart showing monthly accident trends to identify seasonal patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: D3.js line chart with light blue area fill under the line, in a white card
X-axis: month names (Jan-Dec), Y-axis: accident count
Peak month annotated with a badge (e.g., 'Peak: December')
Chart title: 'Monthly Accident Trend', subtitle: 'Aggregated across all years — seasonal patterns'
Data loaded from `public/data/by_month.json`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Analytics page — responsive grid layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want all Analytics charts arranged in a clean grid layout matching the Figma design.
acceptanceCriteria: KPI cards row at top (4 columns)
Second row: Accidents Over Time (left, wider) + Severity Distribution (right, narrower)
Third row: Top 10 Counties (left) + Weather Conditions and Heatmap (right, stacked)
Bottom row: Monthly Accident Trend (full width)
Grid layout uses Tailwind CSS classes
Spacing and proportions match Figma analytics.png
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This story arranges already-built chart components into the final layout

userStories.title: Explorer page — Leaflet map with clustered markers
userStories.description: As a user, I want an interactive map showing accident locations with marker clustering.
acceptanceCriteria: Leaflet.js map with OpenStreetMap tiles, occupying the main content area
Map defaults to SF Bay Area view (center: ~37.77, -122.42, zoom: 12)
Loads map data chunks progressively from `public/data/map/manifest.json` and chunk files
Shows loading indicator with progress while chunks are loading
Marker clustering via leaflet.markercluster: clusters at low zoom, individual markers at high zoom
Marker color reflects severity: green (1), yellow (2), orange (3), red (4)
Map is pannable and zoomable
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Default zoom to SF per user request. Progressive chunk loading with loading indicator.

userStories.title: Explorer page — left filter sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a left sidebar with filters to narrow down accidents by date range, severity, and weather.
acceptanceCriteria: Left sidebar with 'Filters' header and collapse/expand toggle
Date Range section: start date and end date input pickers
Severity section: checkboxes for levels 1-4 with labels (Minor, Moderate, Serious, Fatal)
Weather Condition section: checkboxes for common conditions (Clear, Rain, Fog, Snow, Cloudy, etc.)
Changing any filter updates which markers are visible on the map
Sidebar matches Figma explorer.png layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Explorer page — accident detail panel (right sidebar)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a map marker and see accident details in a right panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a marker opens a right-side panel
Panel shows: Accident Details header, Date & Time, Severity badge, Distance, Description
Panel shows weather info: Temperature, Humidity, Visibility, Wind Speed
When no marker is selected, panel shows 'Click on an accident marker to view details' placeholder
Panel has a close button
Layout matches Figma explorer_selected.png
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Explorer page — timeline slider
userStories.description: As a user, I want a timeline slider at the bottom of the map to filter accidents by date range.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontal range slider at bottom of the map area
Shows start and end date labels
Dragging slider handles filters which markers are displayed by date
Slider range covers full dataset date range
Matches Figma Explorer page bottom bar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: About page
userStories.description: As a user, I want an About page explaining the project, data sources, danger score formula, and team.
acceptanceCriteria: About page renders at /about route
Project description and purpose section
Danger score formula explanation with the formula: (S1*N1 + S2*N2 + S3*N3 + S4*N4) / T
Dataset citation with source links (Kaggle, original papers)
Team member names: Alexander Nikols, Zoe Long, Andra Bhargav Teja
Course info: CSC 805, SFSU
Clean, readable layout with Tailwind styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: User confirmed About page should include danger score formula explanation
